general_news.title: Why Kim Jong Un Won’t Pick Up the Phone and What to Do About It
general_news.summary: Every American administration pledges to learn from the past when devising a strategy for contending with nuclear North Korea. And yet, few portfolios are so paralyzed by path dependency and resistance to learning. general_news.title: What is Strategic Rivalry? Why Should We Care?
general_news.summary: The states most likely to draw America into its next major crisis or war are not unknowns. They are the usual suspects: The same handful of states that have threatened the United States repeatedly across decades. Interstate rivals have caused roughly 80 percent of history’s wars and the odds of any given rivalry ending peacefully are little better than a coin toss.
